revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:石山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ishiyama Kofun | image = {{No image available|cat=Iga City}} | location = [[Saira, Iga City]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.707725|N|136.172942|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (Keyhole-shaped mound) | area = Total mound length: 120m | built = Late 4th century | burial = Clay-encased coffins | burial goods = Stone bracelets, bronze mirrors, armor | designation1 = None }} '''Ishiyama Kofun''' (石山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in {{ill|Saira, Iga City|ja|才良|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saira, Iga City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. It was excavated between 1948 and 1951 by the {{ill|Kyoto University|en|Kyoto University|qid=Q336264|lt=Kyoto University|1=Kyoto University}} Archaeological Research Laboratory under the supervision of {{ill|Kobayashi Yukio|ja|小林行雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobayashi Yukio|qq=}}. This kofun is considered representative of Kofuns constructed in the late 4th century. == Mound area and Haniwa == The mound was constructed using the edge of a floodplain hill, and its name is derived from the significant use of stone facing (''fukiishi''). It measures 120 meters in total length, with a rear circular section diameter of 70 meters and a front rectangular section width of 40 meters. The haniwa arrangement is remarkable, featuring three rows of cylindrical haniwa encircling the mound. Inside the rear mound’s apex is a rectangular arrangement of cylindrical haniwa, surrounded by house-shaped, shield-shaped, lid-shaped, and quiver-shaped (''yatsuko'') haniwa. Additionally, the constriction area outside the mound also features rectangular arrangements of haniwa, with numerous house-shaped haniwa placed there. 石山古墳 家形埴輪 入母屋造.JPG|House-shaped haniwa, hip-and-gable roof
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|en|Kyoto University Museum|qid=Q11374929|lt=Kyoto University Museum|1=Kyoto University Museum}} (same for other images).}} 石山古墳 家形埴輪 寄棟造.JPG|House-shaped haniwa, hip roof 石山古墳 家形埴輪 切妻造-1.JPG|House-shaped haniwa, gable roof 石山古墳 切妻造-2.JPG|House-shaped haniwa, gable roof 石山古墳 家形埴輪 片流れ.JPG|House-shaped haniwa, single-sloped roof 石山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped haniwa 石山古墳 斗束の線刻された家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa with engraved tozukuri details 石山古墳 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa 石山古墳 靫形埴輪.JPG|Quiver-shaped haniwa 石山古墳 朝顔形埴輪・円筒埴輪.JPG|Morning glory-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa == Internal burial and burial goods == The rear mound contains three clay-encased coffins arranged in a shared burial pit. Each coffin contains a wooden coffin: * '''Central Coffin:''' A split-log style coffin, 8.1 meters long. Although partially looted, it contained scale armor, agricultural tools, iron arrowheads, stone replicas of comma-shaped bronze objects, and shields inside and around it. * '''Eastern Coffin:''' Another split-log style coffin, 7.7 meters long, contained shields with comma-shaped bronze objects, quivers holding bronze and iron arrowheads, beads, stone replicas, [[:en:Flower mirror|mirrors with internal floral designs]], combs, swords, scale armor, and agricultural tools. * '''Western Coffin:''' A box-style wooden coffin, 3.7 meters long, held 13 stone diadems, 44 wheel-shaped stones, 13 stone bracelets, stone replicas of musical bridge burial goods, spindle whorls, magatama beads, swords, and spears. == Roles of the Deceased == A significant distinction was noted between the grave goods of the central/eastern coffins and the western coffin. The central and eastern coffins contained numerous weapons and armor, suggesting that the Interred_individuals were political or military leaders. Conversely, the western coffin contained fewer weapons but many stone ornaments, indicating a ceremonial or religious role.Shiraishi (2009), dd. 73–75. == References == * Kobayashi Yukio (ed.), "Ishiyama Kofun," in *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 1974, 7th Edition, dd. 48–49. * Shiraishi Taichiro, "Western Coffin of Ishiyama Kofun," in *The Yamato State from an Archaeological Perspective*, Aoki Shoten, 2009, dd. 73–75. == notes == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shikinzan Kofun|ja|紫金山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinzan Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/rekishi/kenshi/asp/hakken2/detail.asp?record=307 Re-surveying the Mound - Contributions to Archaeological Research] (Mie Prefecture Cultural Promotion Division) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|石山古墳|version=103793890|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[fr:Ishiyama Kofun]] [[ja:石山古墳]] {{Commons category|Ishiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11585523}} [[Category:History of Iga, Mie]] [[Category:Iga Province]] [[Category:Image Requests for Iga]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
